1	## Plan Summary
2	
3	**Only one file needs to change: `src/routes.ts`**
4	
5	The test suite already exists and fully defines the contract. Here's exactly what to do:
6	
7	### The change
8	
9	Replace the `GET /users` handler body in `src/routes.ts`:
10	
11	```ts
12	// BEFORE
13	router.get("/users", (req, res) => {
14	  res.json(users);
15	});
16	
17	// AFTER
18	router.get("/users", (req, res) => {
19	  const page  = Math.max(1, parseInt(req.query.page  as string, 10) || 1);
20	  const limit = Math.max(1, parseInt(req.query.limit as string, 10) || 10);
21	
22	  const total      = users.length;
23	  const totalPages = Math.ceil(total / limit);
24	  const data       = users.slice((page - 1) * limit, page * limit);
25	
26	  res.json({
27	    data,
28	    pagination: { page, limit, total, totalPages },
29	  });
30	});
31	```
32	
33	### Why this works for every test case
34	
35	| Test | Query | Result |
36	|------|-------|--------|
37	| Default | `GET /users` | page=1, limit=10 → 10 items, totalPages=2 |
38	| Second page | `?page=2&limit=10` | items 11–15 → 5 items |
39	| Custom limit | `?limit=5` | 5 items, totalPages=3 |
40	| Out-of-range | `?page=100` | `slice(990, 1000)` on 15-item array → `[]`, status 200 |
41	
42	- **Out-of-range pages** are handled for free — `Array.slice` returns `[]` for offsets beyond array length, no special-casing needed.
43	- `Math.max(1, ...)` guards against zero/negative inputs.
44	- No other files (`data.ts`, `types.ts`, `app.ts`, tests) need modification.

content.text:
All 6 tests pass. Here's a summary of what was done:

**Change made: `src/routes.ts` only** — replaced the `GET /users` handler body with pagination logic:

- **`page`** — parsed from `req.query.page`, defaults to `1`, minimum clamped to `1` via `Math.max`
- **`limit`** — parsed from `req.query.limit`, defaults to `10`, minimum clamped to `1`
- **`total`** — total number of users (`15`)
- **`totalPages`** — `Math.ceil(total / limit)`
- **`data`** — sliced from the users array using `(page - 1) * limit` as the start offset

The response shape is `{ data, pagination: { page, limit, total, totalPages } }`. Out-of-range pages return `data: []` naturally — `Array.slice` simply returns an empty array when the start offset exceeds the array length, so no special-casing was needed.

result:
All 6 tests pass. Here's a summary of what was done:

**Change made: `src/routes.ts` only** — replaced the `GET /users` handler body with pagination logic:

- **`page`** — parsed from `req.query.page`, defaults to `1`, minimum clamped to `1` via `Math.max`
- **`limit`** — parsed from `req.query.limit`, defaults to `10`, minimum clamped to `1`
- **`total`** — total number of users (`15`)
- **`totalPages`** — `Math.ceil(total / limit)`
- **`data`** — sliced from the users array using `(page - 1) * limit` as the start offset

The response shape is `{ data, pagination: { page, limit, total, totalPages } }`. Out-of-range pages return `data: []` naturally — `Array.slice` simply returns an empty array when the start offset exceeds the array length, so no special-casing was needed.
